attractions.d: 60°C thermal pools (+264 63 297 011, R150/person)
Base of Tugela Falls (second highest waterfall) — 4hr return from Sentinel car park
Half-day minimum, deeply moving — R195/person (apartheidmuseum.org)
58km 4x4 scenic circuit, permit from reception R220/vehicle
Orange River drops 56m into an 18km gorge (free with park entry)
Grade 5 white water rafting — Shearwater or Wildside, full day R2000/person
Pristine coral gardens, dugong snorkelling — dhow day trip from Vilanculos
Highest white rhino concentration in southern Kruger — dawn drive
325m climb, 2hr return, bring 2L water minimum
216m world's highest commercial bungee jump (R1250, facevolunteers.com)
African penguins at eye level — R210/person (sanparks.org)
Highest in Namibia (2,573m) — White Lady rock painting, guide compulsory
Otherworldly volcanic rock in vivid reds, blacks and purples
Buffalo, elephant, hippo and lion territory — self-drive
100,000+ Cape fur seals, 120km north of Swakopmund
Southern tip of Cape Peninsula — funicular R75, dramatic ocean cliffs
7hr return hike, requires fitness — panoramic Drakensberg views
9km cliff drive, R50 toll — best sunset route in the Cape
Largest elephant population in Africa — 4x4 self-drive from Kasane gate
Sunset boat cruise — elephant herds swimming, from R850/person at Kasane
Iconic German Lutheran church, free to visit, colonial architecture walk
Old Fort prison — R85/person, powerful history of the struggle era
Sterkfontein Caves tours, UNESCO site — 1hr northwest of Johannesburg
20min from city centre, self-drive kudu and zebra
Ancient camel thorn trees in white clay pan, 1km walk from 4x4 parking
Swim on the edge of the falls — Zambia side, Sep–Dec only
Jackass penguin colony and flamingos, 20km south of Lüderitz
Lion and cheetah territory in remote western Etosha, self-drive loop
Climb before 7am, 45km from Sesriem gate on C27 (free with park entry)
Highest accessible dune near Walvis Bay, sandboarding, free entry
Stranded 500m inland since 1909, accessible by 4x4 from Conception Bay
Accessible dune 5km from gate, half the crowds of Dune 45
Seasonal flamingos — thousands of pink flamingos on flooded pan
549m deep, second largest canyon in the world — sunrise at Hobas lookout by 6:30am
Remarkable wildlife recovery story — lion, elephant, buffalo and hippo
Elephant herds arrive in the late afternoon daily, 4–6pm
Largest African penguin colony in Namibia, viewed from mainland shore
Dramatic canyon rim views, 4x4 track along the Fish River Canyon edge
Hamilton Russell Vineyards, 19km from Hermanus, pinot noir and chardonnay
12km path along cliff, best Aug–Nov for Southern Right whales
Far fewer people than Deadvlei, 2.5km through the pans
White rhino tracking on foot — wilderness trails, book well in advance
Largest elephant population in Zimbabwe — 40,000+ animals at dry season waterholes
Red Kalahari dunes — lion, cheetah and black-maned lion territory
Dramatic estuary entrance between sandstone cliffs — boat trip recommended
Diamond-rush ruins buried in sand, guided tour 08:30 & 10:00, R220/person
Skulls of the Magnificent Seven tuskers — free with camp entry
Morning hippo counts at S114 causeway — resident pod
Canoeing safari on the Zambezi — elephant and buffalo on the banks
Flat white expanse to horizon — quad bikes with Planet Baobab
Resident lion pride drinks here regularly — best at dawn
Gemsbok herds at Rooiputs waterhole — hundreds in dry season (Aug–Oct)
San rock art, black & white rhino tracking on foot — Cecil Rhodes' grave
Cycle or horse through hippo territory — unique Swaziland experience
Township tour — Tommy's Tour Operator (+264 64 403 123), authentic and insightful
Wild dog dens on Chief's Island — 4x4 self-drive, best Oct–Apr
Best curated local crafts (40 Tal Street), open Mon–Sat
Beautiful restored German colonial fort at eastern gate, sunrise photography
Meerkats at dawn — guide meets you at 5:30am, habituated groups
Floodlit at night — lion, rhino and elephant regularly, stay past 10pm
Mokoro trip through lily pads and papyrus — book through Maun operators
Sundowner boat cruise, hippo pods near Sarasungu river lodge
Panoramic hippo and elephant view from the deck — bring binoculars
260-million-year-old fossilised logs lying in open desert
Big 5 in an extinct volcano crater — 3hr drive from Johannesburg
Lesser-visited granite domes east of Spitzkoppe, superb sunrise light
Okavango rapids, 45min west of Divundu, hippo pools below the falls
Black-rated 4x4 tracks, permit from SANParks — most remote park in SA
Thendele Camp cliff-edge sundowner — book dinner at camp ahead
Dunes drop into the Atlantic Ocean, 4x4 essential — Levo Expeditions (levotouring.com)
Highest pass in southern Africa (2,874m) — 4x4 essential, Lesotho border crossing
Best lion and big cat territory in Kruger — dawn drive along H1-4
Cross the Orange River to Namibia on a floating pontoon
30m deep gorge carved by the Tsauchab River, 1km loop walk at sunset
Full moon hike — incredible city lights, go with a group
Mozambique border zone coral reef diving — pristine reefs 1hr north
Walking safaris — birthplace of the guided bush walk in Africa
1,728m granite inselberg — guided summit hike, San rock art at Bushman's Paradise (R150)
Hippo and croc boat cruise — R170/person, departs 8:30am
Cable car (tablemountain.net) — book online, go early to beat queues
Surf fishing — kabeljou, steenbras and galjoen from the beach
77m above Storms River mouth — 1hr walk return through indigenous forest
Second highest waterfall in the world — best seen after good rains
Dawn lion tracking — pride of 8+ patrols the Nossob riverbed
UNESCO World Heritage, 6,000+ petroglyphs, guided tour R200
One of the world's largest waterfalls — Rainforest Trail Zimbabwe side, R400/person
111m bungee jump (Shearwater +263 13 40006, R3500) and gorge swing
Thousands of pink flamingos year-round at the salt works — boat tour from R450
4hr return hike to iconic quartzite arch — permit via CapeNature
Narrow slot canyon scramble — adventurous 2hr route
